args.claim: Early-onset Alzheimer’s disease (EOAD) is typically defined as Alzheimer’s disease with symptom onset before age 65, and it accounts for about 5–10% of all Alzheimer’s cases.
args.quote: Early-onset AD (EOAD) is conventionally defined as AD with symptom onset before 65 years of age and accounts for approximately 5%–10% of all AD cases.
